prompt_text: Open source or affordable finance tools instead of QuickBooks
response:
# Open Source & Affordable Finance Tools

## **Free/Open Source Options**

### Accounting
- **GnuCash** — Desktop app, double-entry bookkeeping, steeper learning curve
- **Wave** — Cloud-based, free invoicing & accounting (ad-supported)
- **Invoice Ninja** — Self-hosted or cloud, invoicing + expenses
- **Akaunting** — Self-hosted accounting, clean interface

### Business-Focused
- **Odoo Community** — ERP suite (requires hosting/technical setup)
- **Frappe ERPNext** — Full ERP, modular
- **Manager.io** — Desktop + cloud option, simple & powerful

---

## **Affordable Paid Options**

| Tool | Price | Best For |
|------|-------|----------|
| **Wave** | Free | Startups, invoicing |
| **Zoho Books** | $9-45/mo | SMBs, multi-currency |
| **Stripe** | Per transaction | Payments only |
| **Square** | Per transaction | Retail/POS |
| **Expensify** | $5+/mo | Expense tracking |
| **FreshBooks** | $15-55/mo | Service businesses |

---

## **Budget-Friendly Setup**

1. **Spreadsheets** (Google Sheets/Excel) + **Wave**
2. **Self-hosted** accounting + **Stripe** for payments
3. **Open source** stack on VPS (~$5-10/mo)

**Which fits your situation?** (solo, team, industry?)
